problems.name: New-onset paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (perioperative)
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Priya Anand (Medicine) / telemetry; confirmed on 12-lead ECG (Cardiology over-read Dr. Steven Marsh)
problems.notes: First documented atrial fibrillation, identified on POD#1 telemetry after urgent open cholecystectomy for perforated cholecystitis. POD#1: three brief (15-20 second) asymptomatic runs. No episodes POD#2-3. POD#4: a single 2-hour asymptomatic episode confirmed by 12-lead ECG, ventricular rate 70-105, hemodynamically tolerated, spontaneously converted to sinus rhythm. Preoperative and current 12-lead ECGs both show normal sinus rhythm. No prior echocardiogram on file. Patient on NO anticoagulation or antiplatelet therapy. Occurred in the setting of acute surgical illness/inflammation — distinction between transient perioperative AF and incident paroxysmal AF is central to management. CHA2DS2-VASc = 4 (age 65-74 = 1, female = 1, hypertension = 1, diabetes = 1).

problems.name: Perforated acute calculous cholecystitis with localized biliary peritonitis (status post urgent open cholecystectomy)
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Helena Vargas (Acute Care Surgery) / ED (Dr. Tomas Reyes)
problems.notes: Presented 6/10/2027 with fevers, chills, and RUQ/epigastric pain. CT abdomen/pelvis: perforated gallbladder with pericholecystic fluid and localized peritonitis. Started on broad-spectrum antibiotics (piperacillin-tazobactam) and taken for urgent OPEN cholecystectomy 6/10 — procedure uncomplicated, gangrenous/perforated gallbladder removed, peritoneal washout. Clinically improving post-op: afebrile since surgery, tolerating liquids, downtrending leukocytosis. Recent major intra-abdominal surgery is directly relevant to bleeding risk and anticoagulation timing.

problems.name: Essential hypertension — poorly controlled
problems.notes: Longstanding hypertension, repeatedly above goal despite three-agent therapy (lisinopril, amlodipine, hydrochlorothiazide). Home BP log and serial PCP visits document systolics frequently 150-168. PCP has escalated therapy several times and counseled on dietary sodium. Contributes 1 point to CHA2DS2-VASc and is also a HAS-BLED factor. Lisinopril and HCTZ held perioperatively; amlodipine continued.

problems.name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
problems.notes: On metformin 1000 mg BID. Most recent outpatient HbA1c 7.6% (4/2027) — suboptimal control; PCP notes partial dietary adherence. No documented retinopathy, nephropathy (normal renal function, eGFR ~68), or known neuropathy. Contributes 1 point to CHA2DS2-VASc. Metformin held perioperatively; managed with sliding-scale insulin inpatient.

problems.name: Gastroesophageal reflux disease
problems.notes: Chronic reflux managed with omeprazole 20 mg daily plus PRN OTC famotidine. No history of GI bleed, no known erosive esophagitis or Barrett's. Relevant to bleeding-risk discussion if anticoagulation is started.

problems.name: Hyperlipidemia
problems.notes: On atorvastatin 40 mg daily; LDL at goal (72, 4/2027). No known coronary, carotid, or peripheral arterial disease (i.e., no 'vascular disease' point for CHA2DS2-VASc).

problems.name: Osteopenia
problems.notes: T-score -2.1 lumbar spine on 2021 DEXA. On calcium + vitamin D. No fragility fractures. No falls in the past year (patient drives independently and is fully ambulatory). Relevant to fall/bleeding-risk framing in an anticoagulation conversation, though fall risk is currently low.

problems.name: Grief / adjustment reaction following spousal loss (improving)
problems.notes: Husband Frank died of pancreatic cancer in 2025; patient was his primary caregiver. PHQ-9 = 8 (mild) in 2025; declined antidepressant, engaged with church grief group; PHQ-9 = 3 by 2027. Documented here because it informs her wariness of aggressive interventions and the importance of shared decision-making. Fully decisional.

reviewedBy: Dr. Priya Anand (PGY-2, Internal Medicine) at POD#4
